rationale: All eight events are Windows Security event 5145 (network share object access) generated by the machine account 'user-01$' from a link-local IPv6 address on the same host, which is consistent with a domain-joined computer performing routine Group Policy refresh. The IPC$/lsarpc accesses (A0, EV-004–EV-007) represent standard MSRPC/LSARPC named-pipe connections used during GP processing, while the SYSVOL accesses (EV-001–EV-003) show the machine reading the default domain policy and default domain controllers policy gpt.ini files — exactly the files read during a normal GP update cycle. No lateral movement indicators, no ADMIN$ or C$ share access, no unusual relative targets, and no process execution evidence are present; the activity is fully explained by periodic Group Policy refresh from the machine itself.
